pgxxix-p1-s1: the sense of ease and comfort which comes at once by taking a few drinks-drinks which they see others taking with impunity.
pgxxix-p1-s2: After they have succumbed to the desire again, as so many do, and the phenomenon of craving develops, they pass through the well-known stages of a spree, emerging remorseful, with a firm resolution not to drink again.
pgxxix-p1-s3: This is repeated over and over, and unless this person can experience an entire psychic change there is very little hope of his recovery.
pgxxix-p1: the sense of ease and comfort which comes at once by taking a few drinks-drinks which they see others taking with impunity. After they have succumbed to the desire again, as so many do, and the phenomenon of craving develops, they pass through the well-known stages of a spree, emerging remorseful, with a firm resolution not to drink again. This is repeated over and over, and unless this person can experience an entire psychic change there is very little hope of his recovery.
pgxxix-p2-s1: On the other hand-and strange as this may seem to those who do not understand-once a psychic change has occurred, the very same person who seemed doomed, who had so many problems he despaired of ever solving them, suddenly finds himself easily able to control his desire for alcohol, the only effort necessary being that required to follow a few simple rules.
pgxxix-p2: On the other hand-and strange as this may seem to those who do not understand-once a psychic change has occurred, the very same person who seemed doomed, who had so many problems he despaired of ever solving them, suddenly finds himself easily able to control his desire for alcohol, the only effort necessary being that required to follow a few simple rules.
pgxxix-p3-s1: Men have cried out to me in sincere and despairing appeal: "Doctor, I cannot go on like this!
pgxxix-p3-s2: I have everything to live for!
pgxxix-p3-s3: I must stop, but I cannot!
pgxxix-p3: Men have cried out to me in sincere and despairing appeal: "Doctor, I cannot go on like this! I have everything to live for! I must stop, but I cannot! You must help me!"
pgxxix-p4-s1: Faced with this problem, if a doctor is honest with himself, he must sometimes feel his own inadequacy.
pgxxix-p4-s2: Although he gives all that is in him, it often is not enough.
pgxxix-p4-s3: One feels that something more than human power is needed to produce the essential psychic change.
pgxxix-p4-s4: Though the aggregate of recoveries resulting from psychiatric effort is considerable, we physicians must admit we have made little impression upon the problem as a whole.
pgxxix-p4-s5: Many types do not respond to the ordinary psychological approach.
pgxxix-p4: Faced with this problem, if a doctor is honest with himself, he must sometimes feel his own inadequacy. Although he gives all that is in him, it often is not enough. One feels that something more than human power is needed to produce the essential psychic change. Though the aggregate of recoveries resulting from psychiatric effort is considerable, we physicians must admit we have made little impression upon the problem as a whole. Many types do not respond to the ordinary psychological approach.
pgxxix-p5-s1: I do not hold with those who believe that alcoholism is entirely a problem of mental control.
pgxxix-p5-s2: I have had many men who had, for example, worked a period of months on some problem or business deal which was to be settled on a certain date, favorably to them.
pgxxix-p5-s3: They took a drink a day or so prior to the date, and then the phenomenon of craving at once became paramount to all other interests so that the
